target.title: feat(heartbeat): add time-of-day schedule for variable intervals
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** Heartbeat uses a single fixed interval (`heartbeat.every`), forcing a tradeoff between responsiveness (low intervals, high token cost) and economy (high intervals, unresponsive during active work).
- **Why it matters:** Users want different heartbeat frequencies at different times of day to align automation costs with actual operational rhythms.
- **What changed:** Added `heartbeat.schedule[]` config array where each entry maps a time-of-day window to an interval. First matching window wins; falls back to `every` when no window matches. Reuses existing timezone infrastructure from `activeHours`.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** Existing configs without `schedule` behave identically. `activeHours` gate, in-flight request deferral, manual wake bypass all preserved. No breaking changes.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #58581
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)

N/A - new feature.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A - new feature with new test coverage:
- `src/infra/heartbeat-schedule.test.ts` (15 tests): window matching, overnight wrap, first-match-wins, timezone, boundary resolution
- `src/config/zod-schema.agent-defaults.test.ts` (6 tests): schema validation for valid/invalid schedule entries
- `src/infra/heartbeat-runner.scheduler.test.ts` (1 test): schedule-based interval in the runner loop

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

New optional config field `heartbeat.schedule`:
```json5
{
  heartbeat: {
    every: "30m",  // fallback
    schedule: [
      { start: "08:00", end: "18:00", every: "15m" },  // work hours
      { start: "18:00", end: "23:00", every: "30m" },  // evening
      { start: "23:00", end: "08:00", every: "2h" },   // overnight
    ],
  }
}
```

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[heartbeat tick] -> fixed intervalMs -> [next tick]

After:
[heartbeat tick] -> resolveScheduleIntervalMs(now) -> [dynamic intervalMs] -> [next tick]
                    |                                                          |
                    +-- also wakes at window boundaries for prompt transitions -+
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS (Darwin 25.3.0)
- Runtime: Node 23.3.0
- Model/provider: N/A (config/scheduling change)

### Steps

1. Add `schedule` array to `heartbeat` config
2. Set system time inside a schedule window
3. Observe heartbeat fires at the window's interval, not the base `every`

### Expected

- Heartbeat uses 15m interval during 08:00-18:00 window
- Heartbeat uses 2h interval during 23:00-08:00 window
- Falls back to 30m when no window matches

### Actual

- Confirmed via unit tests (29 pass) and scheduler integration test (9 pass)

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Test results: 38 tests pass across 4 test files. `pnpm build` and `pnpm check` pass clean.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: schedule window matching, overnight wrap, first-match-wins, timezone resolution, boundary wakeup, fallback to `every`, Zod schema validation
- Edge cases checked: empty schedule array, invalid times, invalid durations, zero-width windows, overlapping entries
- What you did **not** verify: live gateway deployment with real heartbeat runs

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? Yes (new optional `heartbeat.schedule` field)
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Window boundary timer could fire unnecessarily when no schedule is configured
  - Mitigation: `resolveNextWindowBoundaryMs` returns `null` when no schedule exists, so the boundary check is skipped entirely

## AI Disclosure

- [x] This PR was AI-assisted (Claude Code / Opus 4.6)
- [x] Fully tested (38 tests, build, lint all pass)
- [x] Author understands what the code does

target.title: feat(config): add provenance tracking and explain command
target.body:
## Summary

This PR adds an initial config provenance surface to OpenClaw and exposes it through a new CLI command:

- `openclaw config explain`
- `openclaw config explain --json`

The goal is to improve operator/debug UX by showing:
- the active config path
- whether `$include` entries were present
- whether `${ENV}` substitutions were detected
- whether selected high-value keys came from the config file or runtime defaults

## What changed

### Added
- `src/config/provenance.ts`
  - provenance types/helpers
  - tracked high-value config key paths

- `src/commands/config-explain.ts`
  - user-facing command implementation

### Updated
- `src/config/io.ts`
  - attaches provenance metadata to `ConfigFileSnapshot`

- `src/config/types.openclaw.ts`
  - adds optional `provenance` to `ConfigFileSnapshot`

- `src/config/config.ts`
  - re-exports provenance surface

- `src/cli/config-cli.ts`
  - registers `openclaw config explain`

- `docs/cli/config.md`
  - documents the new command

## Why

OpenClaw already has a strong config/runtime system, but it can still be hard to answer questions like:

- which config file was loaded?
- were includes/env substitutions involved?
- did this setting come from the file or from defaults?

This PR adds a first, scoped provenance layer aimed at improving supportability and contributor ergonomics without changing config enforcement behavior.

## Scope / non-goals

This is intentionally a small first slice:
- no full per-key provenance for the entire config tree
- no changes to runtime config enforcement
- no gateway behavior changes
- no sandbox/approval changes

Tracked keys currently include:
- `gateway.mode`
- `agents.defaults.workspace`
- `agents.defaults.model`
- `agents.defaults.sandbox.mode`
- `plugins.entries`
- `cron.enabled`
- `agents.defaults.heartbeat.every`

## Validation note

Automated test execution was blocked in this checkout because repo dependencies are not currently installed (`node_modules` missing, `vitest` unavailable in this environment).

The implementation was kept additive and scoped to:
- config snapshot metadata
- CLI command wiring
- docs

## Follow-up ideas

If this direction is useful, follow-up PRs could extend provenance to:
- richer include-chain reporting
- more selected high-value keys
- config troubleshooting/doctor integration

target.title: fix: show effective session model in dashboard
target.body:
## Summary

- Add a **Model** column to the sessions table so operators can see the per-session effective model at a glance
- Fix transcript model fallback in `buildGatewaySessionRow`: when no runtime model (`entry.model`) or explicit override (`entry.modelOverride`) is recorded but the transcript contains the actual model used, prefer the transcript model over the configured default (`claude-opus-4-6`)

## Root cause

The dashboard sessions table had no model column, making it impossible to see which model each session was actually using. The overview cards showed `s.model` for the top 5 recent sessions, but the sessions table (the primary session management view) omitted model entirely.

Additionally, `buildGatewaySessionRow` would fall back to `DEFAULT_MODEL` ("claude-opus-4-6") when `entry.model` was not set, even when the transcript contained the actual model used. This caused sessions without persisted runtime model data to misleadingly show as Opus.

## Changes

**`src/gateway/session-utils.ts`** — Added `useTranscriptFallback` after the existing `shouldUseTranscriptModelIdentity` logic. When `!runtimeModelPresent && !entry.modelOverride && transcriptUsage?.model` exists, uses the transcript model instead of the configured default. Respects existing priority: entry.model > modelOverride > transcript > default.

**`ui/src/ui/views/sessions.ts`** — Added `shortModelName()` helper (strips provider prefix), `<th>Model</th>` column header, and a `<td>` cell displaying the shortened model name with tooltip. Updated empty-state colspan.

## Test plan

- [x] All 77 `session-utils.test.ts` tests pass (including override precedence tests)
- [x] All 4 `sessions.test.ts` UI tests pass
- [x] PAL codereview: 0 issues
- [x] PAL secaudit: 0 HIGH/CRITICAL (2 LOW — lit-html escaping mitigates XSS, model metadata exposure acceptable for operator dashboard)

target.title: feat(tui): add /upload command for file context
target.body: ## Summary - Problem: No way to interactively browse and attach local files as context in the TUI - Why it matters: Users often need to provide file content for the AI to reference, but currently must copy-paste file path or reference files manually - What changed: Added `/upload` slash command with an interactive file browser overlay that supports directory navigation, multi-select, search/filter, and binary file detection - What did NOT change (scope boundary): No changes to CLI (non-TUI) mode, no changes to message protocol or session handling, no changes to existing commands ## Change Type (select all) - [ ] Bug fix - [x] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [x] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes # - Related # - [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable) N/A ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) N/A ## User-visible / Behavior Changes - New `/upload` TUI slash command available in help text - Interactive file browser overlay with keyboard navigation (arrow keys, Enter, Space, Esc, Backspace) - Multi-select files with checkboxes (Space to toggle) - Search/filter files by typing - Binary files are automatically detected and skipped - Selected file contents are prepended to the next user message with `[Attached files for context]` header ## Diagram (if applicable) ```text /upload -> [File Browser Overlay] -> Space to select files -> Enter to confirm -> Files read & validated (binary skipped) -> Content stored as pendingFileContext -> Next message sent with file content prepended ``` ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? `No` — reads files already accessible to the user process - Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No` - New/changed network calls? `No` - Command/tool execution surface changed? `No` - Data access scope changed? `No` ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: Linux (WSL2) - Runtime/container: Node 22+ - Model/provider: Any - Integration/channel (if any): TUI - Relevant config (redacted): Default ### Steps 1. Run `openclaw` to start the TUI 2. Type `/upload` and press Enter 3. Browse directories, select files with Space, press Enter to confirm 4. Type a message — the selected file content is included automatically ### Expected - File browser overlay appears with current directory listing - Files can be selected/deselected, directories navigated - On confirm, files are attached to the next message ### Actual - Works as expected ## Evidence - [x] Failing test/log before + passing after - [ ] Trace/log snippets - [ ] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) 20 unit tests added in `src/tui/components/file-browser-select-list.test.ts` covering: rendering, navigation, file selection/deselection, directory entry, parent navigation, search/filter, confirm/cancel callbacks, hidden file exclusion, scroll info, and state preservation across navigation. ## Human Verification (required) - Verified scenarios: basic file selection, multi-select, directory navigation, binary file skipping, search/filter, cancel with Esc, file content correctly prepended to next message - Edge cases checked: empty directories, permission errors on unreadable files, binary file detection, deeply nested navigation, dotfiles excluded - What you did **not** verify: behavior on Windows native (tested on WSL2 only), extremely large files (no size cap implemented) ## Review Conversations - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? `Yes` - Config/env changes? `No` - Migration needed? `No` ## Risks and Mitigations - Risk: Large files could cause high memory usage when read with `readFileSync` - Mitigation: Users select files explicitly; binary detection prevents accidental large binary reads. A file size limit could be added as a follow-up if maintainers prefer. --- 🤖 This PR was AI-assisted (Claude). The code has been lightly tested locally and verified with `pnpm build`, `pnpm check`, and `pnpm test` (all passing; 20 new unit tests added). The contributor understands what the code does.

target.body:
## Summary
- add a shared scoped mention-pattern policy for global, agent, and provider/channel overrides
- apply configured mention-pattern gating across channel runtimes while preserving native mentions
- document raw regex-body syntax and regenerate config/help metadata

## Notes
- Recreated from a clean `origin/main` branch after #70846 was closed as dirty; this branch has been rebased on current `origin/main` and generated baselines refreshed after upstream movement.

## Validation
- `pnpm --config.minimum-release-age=0 install --lockfile-only --ignore-scripts=false --config.engine-strict=false --config.enable-pre-post-scripts=true --config.side-effects-cache=true`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- `pnpm config:channels:check`
- `pnpm test extensions/qa-channel/src/inbound.test.ts src/channels/mention-pattern-policy.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.body.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Scoped mention-pattern policy now resolves the effective provider/agent/conversation policy so configured regex mentions are enabled only for explicitly allowed conversations in deny mode and remain disabled for denied or unlisted conversations. Latest follow-up rebased onto current `origin/main`, resolved refreshed generated config/plugin SDK baseline conflicts, and reran the exact-head behavior proof.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw PR checkout on host `patrick`, branch `feature/mention-pattern-policy-clean`, head `99808aff43865c102985cdc348ee6ca824b140e0`, running the real source resolver, Telegram group peer id helper, mention matcher, and inbound mention decision code through Node/tsx against redacted OpenClaw-style config/message facts. This does not exercise live external delivery.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `cd /home/patrick/projects/openclaw-mention-pattern-policy && HEAD_SHA=99808aff43865c102985cdc348ee6ca824b140e0 HEAD_SHORT=99808aff43 node --import tsx /tmp/pr70864-proof-02d093.mjs`
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the real source smoke:

```text
OpenClaw scoped mention policy Telegram account media pre-download proof (99808aff43, 99808aff43865c102985cdc348ee6ca824b140e0)
{"topic":7,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:7","accountPolicyAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:7"],"globalProviderAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:global-only"],"mentionPatternsEnabled":true,"wasMentioned":true,"shouldSkip":false,"skipReason":null}
{"topic":9,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:9","accountPolicyAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:7"],"globalProviderAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:global-only"],"mentionPatternsEnabled":false,"wasMentioned":false,"shouldSkip":true,"skipReason":null}
{"topic":11,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:11","accountPolicyAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:7"],"globalProviderAllowIn":["-1001234567893:topic:global-only"],"mentionPatternsEnabled":false,"wasMentioned":false,"shouldSkip":true,"skipReason":null}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** In inherited `deny` mode, the media pre-download path honors the Telegram account policy rather than the global provider fallback: account-allowed topic `-1001234567893:topic:7` enables configured regex mention `bot` and media pre-download is not skipped, while account-denied topic `:topic:9` and unlisted topic `:topic:11` disable configured regex mentions and skip unaddressed group media before download.
- **What was not tested:** Full live external Telegram delivery was not exercised in this proof; CI and targeted channel tests cover the integration paths.

target.title: fix(memory-host): add AbortSignal support to batch polling (OpenAI, Voyage, Gemini)
target.body:
Add AbortSignal support to batch polling loops to enable immediate cancellation when clients disconnect.

Problem
When HTTP clients disconnect during batch embedding operations, server-side polling continues until timeout, wasting API quota.

Solution
- Replace uncancelable setTimeout with cancellable version using clearTimeout
- Add signal?: AbortSignal parameter to all batch providers
- Use { once: true } to prevent listener accumulation (fixes MaxListenersExceededWarning)
- Thread signal through full call chain: batch-runner → runXxxBatches → waitForXxxBatch

Changes
- batch-runner.ts: Add signal to EmbeddingBatchExecutionParams type
- batch-openai.ts: Add signal support with {once: true} cleanup
- batch-voyage.ts: Update sleep type, add signal threading through deps
- batch-gemini.ts: Add signal support with {once: true} cleanup

Testing
- Verified polling stops within 1s of abort (was 30+ iterations)
- No listener leaks after long polls
- TypeScript compiles without errors

Backwards Compatible: signal is optional.

**Additional Fixes (Post-Review)**:
- Fixed `initial: batchInfo` parameter regression in `batch-voyage.ts` and `batch-gemini.ts` to avoid extra API calls on batch creation
- Fixed locale regression in `ui/src/ui/presenter.ts` (reverted hardcoded "en-US" to `undefined` for i18n compliance)

target.title: fix: use active vision model for image tool fallback
target.body:
## Root cause
The `image` tool only treated `agents.defaults.imageModel` or auto/default media candidates as usable image-capable models. When `agents.defaults.imageModel` was empty, a run whose active model already supported image input could still fail with `No image model is configured` instead of using that active model for explicit image-tool analysis.

## Fix
- Pass the active reply model metadata into `createImageTool`.
- Let image-tool model resolution consider that active model first when it supports image input.
- Preserve explicit `agents.defaults.imageModel`, fallback ordering, provider auth checks, and provider/model ref normalization.

## Verification
- `PATH=/opt/homebrew/opt/node@22/bin:$PATH pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/openclaw-tools.ts src/agents/tools/image-tool.ts src/agents/tools/image-tool.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.tts-config.test.ts`
- `PATH=/opt/homebrew/opt/node@22/bin:$PATH node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/image-tool.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.tts-config.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`

## Coverage
The regression test now focuses on the broken path: an attachment/local image with a vision-capable active model and no `agents.defaults.imageModel`. Existing image-tool tests continue covering data URLs, remote media, schema, sandboxing, provider fallbacks, and explicit image model behavior.

## Live proof
- Local mock provider run with `agents.defaults.imageModel` unset and active model `openai/gpt-5.5`.
- `createImageTool` executed against a real local image and selected `openai/gpt-5.5`.
- Output: `mock-ok openai/gpt-5.5`

target.title: Improve compaction with microCompact pre-pass and analysis scratchpad
target.body:
## Summary

Adopts three techniques to improve compaction quality and reduce LLM cost, addressing #58398:

- **microCompact pre-pass** — Before LLM summarization runs, clears old tool result content from messages whose `toolName` is in the bulky-result set (`read`, `write`, `edit`, `exec`, `bash`, `shell`, `web_search`, `web_fetch`, `browser`). Replaces their content with `[Tool output cleared - not recent]`. The last 5 tool results are preserved (configurable). Error results are never cleared. This is zero LLM cost — pure string replacement on the messages array.

- **Analysis scratchpad in compaction prompt** — Appends instructions asking the model to produce a two-phase response: an `<analysis>` block for chronological reasoning, then a `<summary>` block with the actual summary. The `<analysis>` block is stripped before the summary enters context. This produces better-structured summaries because the model reasons through the conversation before committing to the final output.

- **Post-compact transcript reference** — After compaction, the summary includes a reference to the full session JSONL transcript path so the model can self-serve if it needs details lost in summarization.

## Test plan

- [x] Unit tests for `microCompactMessages` (13 tests) — covers clearable tool names, preserve count, error immunity, metadata preservation, custom preserve count, edge cases
- [x] Unit tests for `stripAnalysisBlock` (8 tests) — covers both-phases present, analysis-only stripping, no-tags fallback, multiline, whitespace trimming
- [x] Unit test for `ANALYSIS_SCRATCHPAD_INSTRUCTIONS` content assertions
- [x] All 146 existing compaction tests pass with zero regressions
- [x] Pre-existing TS errors in `extensions/whatsapp/src/channel.ts` confirmed on baseline (not introduced)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: feat: add before_block_reply plugin hook for block streaming interception
target.body:
## Problem

OpenClaw's block streaming path flushes the reply buffer on every `tool_execution_start` event, causing pre-tool narration text to post as separate Slack messages (fragmentation). The final delivery path may also re-post the same content (duplication). This double-message bug has been a persistent issue across all fleet nodes.

## Solution

Adds a new `before_block_reply` plugin hook that fires before any block reply payload is delivered to a channel. Plugins can:
- **Inspect** the payload and its trigger context
- **Modify** the text (`{ text: '...' }`)
- **Cancel** delivery entirely (`{ cancel: true }`)

Each block reply now carries a `trigger` context field indicating why the flush is happening:
- `tool_start` — narration flushed before a tool call
- `text_end` — natural text block boundary
- `message_end` — final assistant message
- `run_end` — safety flush at end of run

This enables plugins like `slack-plan-block` to intercept tool-start narration and route it into task cards instead of posting as separate messages, while allowing final answers through normally.

## Implementation

- Follows the existing `message_sending` hook pattern (modifying hook with sequential execution)
- `mergeResults` combines text modifications; `cancel` is sticky
- `shouldStop` short-circuits on `cancel=true`
- Trigger context threaded through the entire block reply pipeline (14 files)
- No behavioral changes without a plugin registered — fully backwards compatible

## Files changed (14)

- `src/plugins/types.ts` — hook name, event/result types, handler map
- `src/plugins/hooks.ts` — `runBeforeBlockReply` runner
- `src/auto-reply/types.ts` — `BlockReplyFlushTrigger` type
- `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts` — hook call site in `onBlockReply`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/block-reply-pipeline.ts` — context threading
- `src/auto-reply/reply/reply-delivery.ts` — context threading
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.ts` — context threading
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.types.ts` — signature updates
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts` — `tool_start` trigger
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.messages.ts` — `text_end`/`message_end` triggers
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.lifecycle.ts` — `run_end` trigger
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.types.ts` — signature updates
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/params.ts` — signature updates
- `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` — import update

target.title: fix(exec-approvals): add .catch() to expiry delivery fire-and-forget
target.body:
## Problem

The exec-approval forwarder's expiry timeout handler uses a fire-and-forget async IIFE (`void (async () => { ... })()`) to deliver expiry notifications. If `deliverToTargets` throws an unexpected error that escapes its internal per-target catch, the rejection becomes unhandled. This is the same class of bug fixed in PR #84616 (subagent lifecycle) and PR #82943 (subagent restore).

## Fix

Add `.catch()` with `log.error()` to the existing fire-and-forget IIFE. Production diff is +4 lines.

**Key design decision:** `pending.delete(requestId)` stays BEFORE the `await deliverToTargets(...)`, preserving the existing behavior where expiry finalization is independent from channel delivery latency. The previous version of this PR moved the delete after the await, which ClawSweeper correctly identified as a P1 risk (keeping expired forwarder state alive during delivery). This revised version does not change expiry semantics at all.

**What changed vs. the previous version:**
- Removed: moving `pending.delete` after `await deliverToTargets` (the P1 finding)
- Removed: outer catch that re-deletes on delivery failure (no longer needed since delete happens first)
- Added: `.catch()` on the IIFE to log unexpected errors (safety net)
- Tests: verify delete-before-delivery semantics and error logging

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Fire-and-forget async IIFE in exec-approval expiry handler has no `.catch()`, making unexpected delivery errors become unhandled rejections.

Real environment tested: Linux (Ubuntu 24.04, WSL2), Node.js v26.0.0, OpenClaw built from source (commit 46c8864048) at `/tmp/fix-83106`. Gateway started via `node openclaw.mjs gateway` to verify the patched exec-approval forwarder loads in production.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
# 1. Build the patched OpenClaw from source
cd /tmp/fix-83106
CI=true pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
pnpm build

# 2. Verify the .catch() is present in compiled production code
grep -B2 -A5 "failed to deliver expiry notification" dist/server-aux-handlers-*.js

# 3. Start the gateway to confirm the patched forwarder module loads
timeout 8 node openclaw.mjs gateway

# 4. Run the focused test suite
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/exec-approval-forwarder.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix: terminal output copied below.

### Compiled production code verification

The `.catch()` handler is present in the compiled exec-approval forwarder bundle:

```console
$ grep -B2 -A5 "failed to deliver expiry notification" dist/server-aux-handlers-Dug4CJo6.js
});
})().catch((err) => {
log.error(`exec approvals: failed to deliver expiry notification for ${requestId}: ${String(err)}`);
});
}, expiresInMs);
timeoutId.unref?.();
```

### Gateway startup proof

The patched exec-approval forwarder loads successfully in the production gateway:

```console
$ timeout 8 node openclaw.mjs gateway
2026-05-21T11:10:25.297-07:00 [gateway] loading configuration...
2026-05-21T11:10:25.347-07:00 [gateway] starting...
2026-05-21T11:10:28.532-07:00 [gateway] agent model: openai/gpt-5.5 (thinking=medium, fast=off)
2026-05-21T11:10:28.533-07:00 [gateway] http server listening (8 plugins; 3.2s)
2026-05-21T11:10:29.017-07:00 [gateway] ready
2026-05-21T11:10:32.981-07:00 [shutdown] completed cleanly in 79ms
```

### Test suite (22 tests)

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/exec-approval-forwarder.test.ts
 ✓ |infra| exec-approval-forwarder.test.ts (22 tests) 35ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  22 passed (22)
```

### Key test: delete-before-delivery semantics

The test "deletes pending entry before starting expiry delivery" verifies that `pending.delete(requestId)` runs before `deliverToTargets`. During expiry delivery, it calls `handleResolved` on the same request; since the pending entry is already gone, no resolved notice is delivered (only 1 delivery call for the expiry notification):

```typescript
// During expiry delivery, handleResolved finds no pending entry
// because pending.delete ran before deliverToTargets
expect(deliver).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1); // only the expiry notification
```

Observed result after fix: The `.catch()` handler prevents unhandled rejections from the expiry delivery IIFE. The `pending.delete` stays before the await, keeping expiry finalization independent from channel delivery latency. All 22 tests pass (19 existing + 3 new).

What was not tested: Triggering an actual unexpected error that escapes `deliverToTargets`'s internal per-target catch in a live gateway session. The `.catch()` is a safety net for edge cases (like module initialization errors or future code changes that add throwing paths before `deliverToTargets`). The `deliverToTargets` function catches all per-target delivery errors internally, so under normal operation the outer `.catch()` does not fire.

## ClawSweeper P1 fix

**[P1] Finalize expiry before awaiting delivery** (exec-approval-forwarder.ts:577-582): Fixed. The previous version moved `pending.delete(requestId)` after `await deliverToTargets(...)`, keeping expired forwarder state alive during delivery. This version keeps the delete before the await (matching current main) and only adds `.catch()` to the fire-and-forget IIFE for unhandled rejection safety.

target.title: fix(infra): skip shell env fallback probe on Windows
target.body:
## Summary
- Skip the login-shell environment probe entirely on `win32` so native Windows startup does not try to spawn `/bin/sh`.
- Thread an optional platform override through the shell-env fallback for deterministic coverage.
- Add regression coverage that verifies Windows fallback returns successfully without invoking the shell probe or logging the ENOENT warning.

Fixes #84795

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: On native Windows, enabling the shell env fallback could call the login-shell probe and warn with `spawnSync /bin/sh ENOENT` because `/bin/sh` does not exist there.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, branch `fix/windows-shell-env-fallback-skip-84795`, with Vitest simulating `platform: "win32"` through the shell-env fallback path.

Exact steps or command run after fix:

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/shell-env.test.ts
$ git diff --check
```

Evidence after fix:

```console
✓  infra  ../../src/infra/shell-env.test.ts (21 tests) 70ms
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  21 passed (21)
```

Observed result after fix: The new regression test `skips shell env fallback probing on win32` passes. It exercises `loadShellEnvFallback({ enabled: true, platform: "win32" })` with an exec stub that would throw `spawnSync /bin/sh ENOENT`; after the fix, the exec stub is not called, no warning is logged, and the fallback returns `{ ok: true, applied: [] }`.

What was not tested: I did not boot a native Windows host locally. The platform-specific behavior is covered with the same platform override pattern already used by the existing `getShellPathFromLoginShell` Windows test in this file; Windows CI should cover the native runtime environment.

## Test plan
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/shell-env.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Duplicate check
- Checked open PRs for `84795`, `"spawnSync /bin/sh ENOENT"`, `"shell env fallback" "Windows"`, `"probeLoginShellEnv" "win32"`, and `"OPENCLAW_LOAD_SHELL_ENV" "win32"` before publishing.

target.title: fix(runtime): clear orphaned reply run aborts
target.body:
## Summary

- Clear reply-run registry state immediately when aborting a running operation that has no attached backend handle.
- Add a regression QA scenario for the visible-partial-reply stall shape where follow-up messages must drain without an extra user nudge.

## Context

This is aimed at the failure mode in #84903 / #84755 where the transport remains healthy, but a stale interactive run/session lane leaves later messages queued or unprocessed.

## Tests

- Not run: this scratch checkout did not have dependencies installed; pnpm exec vitest attempted an install and failed on registry metadata for @copilotkit/aimock missing the time field.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram/direct main emitted a visible partial reply, then the interactive run stopped making progress and the same session did not drain a follow-up message until a later user nudge. The patch targets the stale lane shape where a reply operation remains running after its attached backend handle is gone.
- Real environment tested: OpenClaw 2026.5.12 running in Docker on NAS, Telegram direct session `agent:main:telegram:direct:8598151747`; patch prepared against current upstream source in the same OpenClaw workspace.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: inspected the patched reply-run registry path and compared it against upstream; attempted the focused vitest command for `src/auto-reply/reply/reply-run-registry.test.ts`; verified the PR head and issue comment through GitHub API.
- Evidence after fix:

```text
$ diff -u upstream/src/auto-reply/reply/reply-run-registry.ts patched/src/auto-reply/reply/reply-run-registry.ts
-      if (phaseBeforeAbort === "queued") {
+      if (phaseBeforeAbort === "queued" || !getAttachedBackend(operation)) {
         clearState();
       }

$ pnpm --config.resolution-mode=highest exec vitest --run src/auto-reply/reply/reply-run-registry.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit.config.ts

Issue field report present: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/84903#issuecomment-4509844878
```

- Observed result after fix: The patched abort path now clears reply-run registry state immediately when the operation has no attached backend handle, instead of waiting for backend cleanup that can no longer run. The added regression test covers an orphaned running operation and asserts `isReplyRunActiveForSessionId(...) === false` after abort.

target.title: test(cli): cover node daemon lifecycle helpers
target.body:
## Summary
- Add focused tests for the node daemon CLI helper surface.
- Cover the already-installed `node install --json` short-circuit so it does not rebuild or reinstall the service.
- Cover `node status --json` payload construction from the resolved node service.

Refs #83924

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: the node daemon lifecycle helper file had no direct tests for service install/status behavior. This left paths such as the already-installed install short-circuit and JSON status payload construction uncovered.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, Node.js v22.22.0, branch `test/node-cli-daemon-lifecycle-83924`, using the real `src/cli/node-cli/daemon.ts` implementation with mocked service/config boundaries under the repo's Vitest wrapper.

Exact steps or command run after fix:

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/node-cli/daemon.test.ts src/cli/node-cli/register.test.ts
$ git diff --check
```

Evidence after fix: Targeted test output:

```console
✓ cli ../../src/cli/node-cli/daemon.test.ts (2 tests) 7ms
✓ cli ../../src/cli/node-cli/register.test.ts (1 test) 2ms

Test Files 2 passed (2)
Tests 3 passed (3)
```

Observed result after fix: The new daemon tests verify `runNodeDaemonInstall({ json: true })` skips install planning and service installation when the node service is already loaded, and verify `runNodeDaemonStatus({ json: true })` emits a JSON payload containing loaded service metadata, command details, and runtime status.

What was not tested: I did not exercise real launchd/systemd/schtasks commands; the tests intentionally mock the service boundary to cover CLI decision logic without installing or managing a real service.

## Test plan
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/node-cli/daemon.test.ts src/cli/node-cli/register.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

commands.author: rowbotik

target.title: Add progress draft label ellipsis option
target.body:
# Add optional ellipsis rendering for progress draft labels

## Summary

Adds an opt-in `streaming.progress.ellipsis` setting for progress draft labels:

- `"off"` keeps current behavior and renders the selected label unchanged.
- `"static"` appends `...` to the progress label.
- `"animated"` cycles the label through no suffix, `.`, `..`, and `...`.

Telegram progress drafts also refresh while an animated progress-only draft is active, so the ellipsis moves even when no new tool-progress lines arrive. The refresh timer is cleared when the draft is reset, finalized, superseded, or the dispatch exits.

Example:

```json5
channels: {
  telegram: {
    streaming: {
      mode: "progress",
      progress: {
        label: "auto",
        ellipsis: "animated"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

## Why

Progress draft labels such as `Working`, `Shelling`, or custom label pools can feel static during long tool calls. This adds a small renderer-level option so users can get classic loading punctuation without baking dots into their configured label list.

Keeping this at render time preserves reusable labels and avoids forcing all users into animated edits.

## Notes

- Default remains `"off"` to preserve current behavior and avoid extra message edits.
- Shared formatter support applies to all channels that use `formatChannelProgressDraftText`.
- True timer-driven animation is currently implemented for Telegram, where progress mode edits a single draft message.
- Other channels will render the configured ellipsis when progress text is re-rendered; they can add timer-driven refreshes later if desired.
- Labels that already end in `.`, `..`, `...`, or `…` are not double-suffixed.

## Testing

- `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.test.ts`
- `pnpm run config:schema:check`
- `pnpm run tsgo:test:src`

## Real Behavior Proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** The new `streaming.progress.ellipsis: "animated"` Telegram progress draft option should visibly cycle the draft label suffix during a long-running turn and stop once the final answer is delivered.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw gateway on macOS with the Telegram channel enabled, Telegram Desktop viewing the bot conversation, and the patched progress draft renderer active.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Configured Telegram progress streaming with `streaming.mode: "progress"`, `progress.label: "auto"`, and `progress.ellipsis: "animated"`; restarted the gateway; sent a Telegram request that triggered a long-running Codex/tool turn; observed the progress draft through final delivery.

**Evidence after fix:** Redacted Telegram Desktop GIF: ![Telegram progress ellipsis proof](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rowbotik/openclaw/proof-progress-label-ellipsis/telegram-progress-ellipsis.gif)

**Observed result after fix:** The Telegram progress draft label visibly cycles through the ellipsis states while the turn is running, then the progress draft is cleared/stopped when the final answer is delivered.

**What was not tested:** Timer-driven animation on non-Telegram channels; those channels still use the shared formatter when they re-render progress text, but this PR only adds active refresh behavior for Telegram.

target.title: fix: resolve QMD Windows shims and guard image URL downloads
target.body:
## Summary

Fix two Windows / provider-compatibility issues:

1. QMD binary resolution on Windows when `memory.qmd.command` resolves to a `.cmd` shim instead of a native executable.
2. OpenAI-compatible image generation responses that return `data[].url` instead of `data[].b64_json`.

The QMD issue was found with QMD installed via:

```bash
npm install -g @tobilu/qmd
```

In the affected Windows environment, the working `qmd` command was provided by a `.cmd` wrapper like:

```bat
@echo off
node "E:\software\encoding\common\nodeJs\node_global\node_modules\@tobilu\qmd\dist\cli\qmd.js" %*
```

Running this wrapper from the shell worked normally, but OpenClaw's Windows wrapper resolver only handled `%~dp0`-relative shim targets. Quoted absolute JS/EXE entrypoints were skipped, so QMD availability checks failed closed with:

```text
qmd.CMD wrapper resolved, but no executable/Node entrypoint could be resolved without shell execution
```

This PR allows Windows `.cmd` wrappers to resolve quoted absolute JS/EXE entrypoints without enabling shell execution.

It also updates QMD package fallback resolution from `qmd` to the actual npm package name, `@tobilu/qmd`.

For image generation, some OpenAI-compatible providers such as NewAPI return generated images as hosted URLs:

```json
{
  "data": [
    {
      "url": "https://provider.example/generated-image.png"
    }
  ]
}
```

OpenClaw previously only parsed `data[].b64_json`, so these responses produced no generated image assets.

This PR adds support for `data[].url` while routing provider-controlled URL downloads through guarded provider HTTP.

## Changes

### QMD / Windows

- Support quoted absolute Windows entrypoints in `.cmd` shim parsing
- Preserve the existing `%~dp0`-relative shim handling
- Apply the same resolver fix to both:
  - plugin SDK Windows spawn resolver
  - memory host SDK Windows spawn resolver
- Use `@tobilu/qmd` for QMD package fallback resolution
- Add unit coverage for `.cmd` shims that quote an absolute JavaScript entrypoint

### OpenAI-compatible image generation

- Parse OpenAI-compatible image responses that include `data[].url`
- Prefer `data[].b64_json` when both `b64_json` and `url` are present
- Download URL-based image assets and infer MIME/file extension from response headers and image bytes
- Route provider-returned image URL downloads through `fetchWithTimeoutGuarded(...)`
- Preserve provider HTTP guardrails for:
  - timeout
  - SSRF/private-network policy
  - dispatcher/proxy policy
  - audit context
  - guarded response release handling
- Add unit coverage proving URL downloads use guarded provider HTTP instead of raw fetch

## Security

Provider-controlled image URLs are not fetched with raw `fetch`.

The follow-up URL download path uses `fetchWithTimeoutGuarded(...)` and carries the same provider HTTP controls used for image generation requests:

- request timeout
- SSRF policy
- private-network opt-in
- dispatcher/proxy policy
- audit context
- guarded response release handling

This avoids bypassing SSRF/private-network policy, proxy/dispatcher policy, and timeout handling.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: QMD Windows `.cmd` shim resolution for quoted absolute JavaScript entrypoints and scoped `@tobilu/qmd` fallback resolution.
- Real environment tested: Windows_NT 10.0.22631, Node v24.9.0, branch `fix/qmd-newapi-real-proof`, repo `E:\sourceCode\java\my\openclaw\openclaw`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a local Node/tsx proof script from `E:\sourceCode\java\my\openclaw\openclaw` that created a temporary Windows-style `node_modules\.bin\qmd.cmd` wrapper pointing at a quoted absolute `node_modules\@tobilu\qmd\dist\cli.js` entrypoint, then called `resolveWindowsSpawnProgram(...)`, `materializeWindowsSpawnProgram(...)`, and `resolveCliSpawnInvocation(...)`.
- Evidence after fix:
  
  ```json
  {
    "cwd": "E:\\sourceCode\\java\\my\\openclaw\\openclaw",
    "platform": "win32",
    "node": "v24.9.0",
    "shimPath": "C:\\Users\\JiaLe\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\openclaw-qmd-proof-oOIzQo\\node_modules\\.bin\\qmd.cmd",
    "shimContainsAbsoluteJsEntrypointWithoutDp0": true,
    "packageName": "@tobilu/qmd",
    "program": {
      "command": "E:\\software\\encoding\\common\\nodeJs\\node.exe",
      "leadingArgv": [
        "C:\\Users\\JiaLe\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\openclaw-qmd-proof-oOIzQo\\node_modules\\@tobilu\\qmd\\dist\\cli.js"
      ],
      "resolution": "node-entrypoint",
      "windowsHide": true
    },
    "invocation": {
      "command": "E:\\software\\encoding\\common\\nodeJs\\node.exe",
      "argv": [
        "C:\\Users\\JiaLe\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\openclaw-qmd-proof-oOIzQo\\node_modules\\@tobilu\\qmd\\dist\\cli.js",
        "--version"
      ],
      "resolution": "node-entrypoint",
      "windowsHide": true
    },
    "cliInvocation": {
      "command": "E:\\software\\encoding\\common\\nodeJs\\node.exe",
      "argv": [
        "C:\\Users\\JiaLe\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\openclaw-qmd-proof-oOIzQo\\node_modules\\@tobilu\\qmd\\dist\\cli.js",
        "--help"
      ],
      "resolution": "node-entrypoint",
      "windowsHide": true
    },
    "shellFallbackUsed": false
  }
  ```
- Observed result after fix: The quoted absolute `.cmd` shim entrypoint resolves to direct Node execution of the `@tobilu/qmd` JavaScript entrypoint, and `shellFallbackUsed` is `false`.
- What was not tested: Full live QMD memory indexing/search workflow and macOS were not tested in this proof run. Image URL guarded fetching is covered by targeted unit tests and code inspection rather than this Windows runtime proof.

## Image generation validation

Validated the OpenAI-compatible URL-response parser and guarded download path with targeted tests.

Before this fix, OpenClaw parsed only `data[].b64_json`, causing URL-only image responses to fail with no generated image assets.

After this fix:

- URL-only OpenAI-compatible image responses produce generated image assets
- `b64_json` remains preferred when present
- URL downloads go through guarded provider HTTP instead of raw `fetch`

For the OpenAI-compatible image URL path, I also ran `image_generate` locally through the configured OpenAI-compatible image provider after this patch.
Evidence after fix:
```text
Generated 1 image with openai/gpt-image-2.

Observed result after fix: URL-backed OpenAI-compatible image generation completed successfully and produced a generated image asset in the local OpenClaw setup.


## Testing

```bash
pnpm exec vitest run src/plugin-sdk/windows-spawn.test.ts
```

```bash
pnpm exec vitest run src/image-generation/image-assets.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/windows-spawn.test.ts
```

Result:

```text
2 test files passed
10 tests passed
```

```bash
pnpm exec vitest run extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts -t "resolves bare qmd command through the scoped QMD package fallback on Windows"
```

Result:

```text
1 test passed
```

```bash
pnpm format:check src/image-generation/image-assets.ts src/image-generation/image-assets.test.ts src/image-generation/openai-compatible-image-provider.ts extensions/openai/image-generation-provider.ts src/plugin-sdk/windows-spawn.ts src/plugin-sdk/windows-spawn.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/qmd-process.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/windows-spawn.ts
```

Result:

```text
All matched files use the correct format.
```

```bash
git diff --check
pnpm tsgo:core
pnpm tsgo:extensions
```

Result:

```text
passed
```

## Platform notes

The QMD wrapper-resolution change has been validated on Windows.

macOS was not tested. The changed QMD wrapper-resolution path is Windows-only (`platform === "win32"`), and non-Windows platforms continue to use the existing direct command path.

The OpenAI-compatible image response parsing change is platform-independent.

target.title: fix(memory): skip placeholder short-term promotions
target.body:
## Summary
- Add a shared short-term promotion guard for markdown placeholder/skeleton snippets.
- Apply it when loading the recall store, recording recall/grounded candidates, ranking, relocating, applying, and rehydrating promotion candidates.
- Add regression coverage for marker-only snippets, heading-plus-empty-bullet skeletons, normalized marker-plus-heading placeholders, stale stored placeholders, direct apply rejection, and inline heading bullets with durable payloads.

Fixes #80582.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.test.ts -- --runInBand`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.test.ts -- --runInBand`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.ts extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Short-term promotion could treat markdown placeholders like `-`, heading-plus-empty-bullet skeletons, and previously persisted normalized skeleton snippets like `- ## Entscheidungen` as durable memory candidates, allowing empty daily-note scaffolding to rank and eventually append to `MEMORY.md`. The guard also preserves list-wrapped heading content with an inline payload, such as `- ## Decision: Move backups to S3`.

Real environment tested: Local filesystem-backed OpenClaw memory workspace on macOS, using PR head `17b7236734`. The standalone Node run created real temp files under `memory/`, wrote `memory/.dreams/short-term-recall.json`, called the production `rankShortTermPromotionCandidates` and `applyShortTermPromotions` functions from `extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.ts`, and checked the resulting `MEMORY.md` filesystem state.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: From the repo root, ran `node --import tsx -` with a standalone script that seeds one temp workspace with a persisted short-term recall entry whose snippet is `- ## Entscheidungen` and another whose snippet is `- ## Decision: Move backups to S3`, then invokes production ranking and apply paths with thresholds set to zero.

Evidence after fix: Copied terminal output from that real temp-workspace run:

```console
$ node --import tsx -
[
  {
    "label": "placeholder",
    "snippet": "- ## Entscheidungen",
    "rankedCandidateCount": 0,
    "appliedCount": 0,
    "memoryMdExists": false,
    "memoryContainsSnippet": false
  },
  {
    "label": "durable",
    "snippet": "- ## Decision: Move backups to S3",
    "rankedCandidateCount": 1,
    "appliedCount": 1,
    "memoryMdExists": true,
    "memoryContainsSnippet": true
  }
]
```

Observed result after fix: The normalized stale placeholder `- ## Entscheidungen` was removed from the promotable path before ranking, applied zero promotions, and did not create `MEMORY.md`. The durable inline heading bullet `- ## Decision: Move backups to S3` ranked, applied once, created `MEMORY.md`, and appeared in the resulting memory file.

What was not tested: I did not run a full live dreaming cron cycle against a private real workspace; the user-reported failure shape is covered with filesystem-backed promotion fixtures and the standalone temp-workspace OpenClaw promotion run above rather than private memory contents.

target.title: fix(cron): accept unknown payload.kind in persisted store validation
target.body:
## Summary

- `getInvalidPersistedCronJobReason` in `src/cron/persisted-shape.ts` rejected any `payload.kind` that was not exactly `"systemEvent"` or `"agentTurn"`, silently dropping cron jobs with legacy kinds (e.g. `"command"`, `"agentmessage"`) from the loaded set on every store reload cycle.
- Relax the validation to only reject missing or non-string `payload.kind` values. Known kinds (`systemEvent`, `agentTurn`) retain their structural validation; unknown kinds pass through so that `doctor-cron-store-migration` can normalize them later.
- This is a **preservation-only fix**: legacy-kind jobs survive store reload but are not yet converted to runnable canonical payloads by the existing doctor migration. A separate follow-up can add migration for specific legacy kinds.
- What did NOT change: schedule validation, payload object presence check, `systemEvent` text validation, `agentTurn` message validation, store reload cycle, sessionTarget defaulting logic, runtime execution path.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security
- [ ] Chore

## Scope

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Cron jobs with legacy `payload.kind` values (e.g. `"command"`, `"agentmessage"`) were silently dropped from the loaded set because the persisted store validation only accepted `"systemEvent"` or `"agentTurn"`. Three production cron jobs were lost for 12 days before discovery.

**Real environment tested:** Node 22.x on Linux, `node --import tsx` against the actual `getInvalidPersistedCronJobReason` and `normalizeCronJobInput` functions from source, simulating the full store reload cycle.

**Exact steps or command run after the patch:** Ran `node --import tsx` simulating the `ensureLoaded` code path: feed 4 jobs (command, agentmessage, systemEvent, missing-kind) through `normalizeCronJobInput` → `getInvalidPersistedCronJobReason` → check which survive into the in-memory store.

**Evidence after fix:** Console output from `node --import tsx` running the actual production code path:

    Simulating cron store reload cycle:

      Loaded into in-memory store:
        cron-command (payload.kind: command)
        cron-agentmsg (payload.kind: agentmessage)
        cron-systemevent (payload.kind: systemEvent)

      Dropped (invalid):
        cron-invalid (payload.kind: (none)) -> invalid-payload

    Result: legacy jobs (command, agentmessage) now survive reload.
    Jobs with missing payload.kind are still rejected.

**Observed result after the fix:** Legacy payload kinds `"command"` and `"agentmessage"` now survive the full normalize → validate → load cycle. Known kinds retain structural validation. Jobs with missing `payload.kind` are still correctly rejected.

**What was not tested:** Live cron store file write/read cycle on disk; `doctor --fix` migration of legacy kinds to canonical forms; runtime execution of non-canonical payload kinds.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: `getInvalidPersistedCronJobReason` used a strict two-kind whitelist that rejected all legacy/custom payload kinds, causing `ensureLoaded` to silently skip them via the `continue` at `src/cron/service/store.ts:104`.
- Missing detection: no allowlist extensibility or passthrough for unknown-but-valid kinds; `saveCronStore` then persisted the filtered in-memory set, making the data loss durable.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: unit test
- Target test file: `src/cron/persisted-shape.test.ts` (new, 10 test cases)
- Scenario: legacy kinds (`command`, `agentmessage`) and arbitrary string kinds return `null` (valid); missing/empty/numeric kinds return `"invalid-payload"`; known kinds retain structural checks.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: locks the relaxed validation contract without requiring a live cron store.

## Compatibility Note

This PR is intentionally **preservation-only**: it prevents data loss by allowing legacy-kind jobs to survive the store reload cycle, but does not add execution or migration for non-canonical payload kinds. The existing `doctor-cron-store-migration` can be extended in a follow-up to convert specific legacy kinds to runnable canonical payloads.

Closes #84922

target.title: fix(codex): stabilize heartbeat dynamic tool schema
target.body:
## Summary

- Register a durable Codex dynamic tool schema set separately from the tools actually callable on the current turn.
- Keep `heartbeat_respond` in the thread schema so normal -> heartbeat -> normal turns reuse the same Codex thread, while normal turns still deny heartbeat tool execution explicitly.
- Keep prompt-facing tool manifests, system prompt reports, context-engine available-tool sets, and trajectory context scoped to tools available for the current turn.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts -t "durable tool schema|registers heartbeat response durably"`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/codex`
- `pnpm build`
- `pnpm check:changed` reached extension typecheck + extension test typecheck, then failed on pre-existing/unrelated OpenRouter lint in `extensions/openrouter/provider-routing.ts` (`unicorn/no-useless-fallback-in-spread`).

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Codex thread rotation caused by per-turn dynamic tool surface changes when heartbeat turns expose `heartbeat_respond` and normal turns do not.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw gateway launched by `ai.openclaw.gateway` on macOS, serving Telegram topic `agent:main:telegram:group:-1003774691294:topic:3731` through Codex app-server and Lossless Claw thread-bootstrap projection.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Checked out the PR branch in `/Users/phaedrus/Projects/clawdbot`, built it, restarted the launchd gateway with `launchctl kickstart -k gui/$(id -u)/ai.openclaw.gateway`, then exercised repeated normal Telegram turns while tailing `/tmp/openclaw/openclaw-2026-05-20.log`. Gateway heartbeat scheduler/diagnostic activity was also present after deploy, but no post-deploy `heartbeat_respond` tool call was found in the session trajectory.

Evidence after fix:

- Deployed build metadata: `dist/build-info.json` reported commit `03649470fdfff82d0b8c0898ff0b15cec9f54247`, the original patch commit on this PR branch before the later merge-from-main commit `2928e127167f974005814c01f56e8a71a5c22aa8`.
- Runtime log at `2026-05-20T15:56:26-04:00`: topic 3731 used `reason=matching-thread-bootstrap-binding`, `projected=false`, previous thread `019e46f3-e61a-75d0-81b1-607def496ca7`.
- Runtime log at `2026-05-20T15:58:01-04:00`: same topic/session again used `reason=matching-thread-bootstrap-binding`, `projected=false`, same thread `019e46f3-e61a-75d0-81b1-607def496ca7`.
- Runtime log at `2026-05-20T16:03:35-04:00`: after gateway restart noise, same topic/session still resumed with `reason=matching-thread-bootstrap-binding`, `projected=false`, same thread `019e46f3-e61a-75d0-81b1-607def496ca7`.
- Runtime log at `2026-05-20T16:14:55-04:00`: same topic/session again resumed with `reason=matching-thread-bootstrap-binding`, `projected=false`, same thread `019e46f3-e61a-75d0-81b1-607def496ca7`.
- Runtime log scan from `15:55-16:39 EDT`: `dynamic-tools-mismatch=0` and no `codex app-server dynamic tool catalog changed; starting a new thread` entries after the patched build went live.
- The only later topic 3731 rotation at `2026-05-20T16:39:25-04:00` was `reason=context-engine-binding-mismatch` after the Lossless Claw epoch changed from `summary-prefix-v1:4754:a60c266b95ba844fd46671d42e665cb8` to `summary-prefix-v1:4754:9699cf92cc713a8b38bd03669b36f3a7`; that is expected context-engine rotation, not dynamic-tool flapping.

Observed result after fix: repeated normal Telegram turns no longer produced `dynamic-tools-mismatch` rotations. The active Telegram topic reused the same Codex thread across repeated turns until an expected context-engine epoch change forced a separate rotation. Post-deploy heartbeat scheduler activity was observed, but a post-deploy heartbeat turn with `heartbeat_respond` was not found in the trajectory, so this proof does not claim a full live heartbeat-turn execution.

Current PR-head validation: After merging current `origin/main` into the branch, CI passed on head `2928e127167f974005814c01f56e8a71a5c22aa8`. Local review validation also passed targeted Codex tests, extension lint, test types, build, and oxlint shards.

What was not tested: A live gateway deployment of the post-merge commit `2928e127167f974005814c01f56e8a71a5c22aa8`, or a confirmed post-deploy heartbeat turn that called `heartbeat_respond`; the live proof was captured on the same patch before the merge-from-main commit, and CI/local validation covers the current PR head.

target.title: fix(exec-approvals): add .catch() to expiry delivery fire-and-forget
target.body:
## Problem

The exec-approval forwarder's expiry timeout handler uses a fire-and-forget async IIFE (`void (async () => { ... })()`) to deliver expiry notifications. If `deliverToTargets` throws an unexpected error that escapes its internal per-target catch, the rejection becomes unhandled. This is the same class of bug fixed in PR #84616 (subagent lifecycle) and PR #82943 (subagent restore).

## Fix

Add `.catch()` with `log.error()` to the existing fire-and-forget IIFE. Production diff is +4 lines.

**Key design decision:** `pending.delete(requestId)` stays BEFORE the `await deliverToTargets(...)`, preserving the existing behavior where expiry finalization is independent from channel delivery latency. The previous version of this PR moved the delete after the await, which ClawSweeper correctly identified as a P1 risk (keeping expired forwarder state alive during delivery). This revised version does not change expiry semantics at all.

**What changed vs. the previous version:**
- Removed: moving `pending.delete` after `await deliverToTargets` (the P1 finding)
- Removed: outer catch that re-deletes on delivery failure (no longer needed since delete happens first)
- Added: `.catch()` on the IIFE to log unexpected errors (safety net)
- Tests: verify delete-before-delivery semantics and error logging

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Fire-and-forget async IIFE in exec-approval expiry handler has no `.catch()`, making unexpected delivery errors become unhandled rejections.

Real environment tested: Linux (Ubuntu 24.04, WSL2), Node.js v26.0.0, OpenClaw built from source (commit 46c8864048) at `/tmp/fix-83106`. Gateway started via `node openclaw.mjs gateway` to verify the patched exec-approval forwarder loads in production.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
# 1. Build the patched OpenClaw from source
cd /tmp/fix-83106
CI=true pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
pnpm build

# 2. Verify the .catch() is present in compiled production code
grep -B2 -A5 "failed to deliver expiry notification" dist/server-aux-handlers-*.js

# 3. Start the gateway to confirm the patched forwarder module loads
timeout 8 node openclaw.mjs gateway

# 4. Run the focused test suite
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/exec-approval-forwarder.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix: terminal output copied below.

### Compiled production code verification

The `.catch()` handler is present in the compiled exec-approval forwarder bundle:

```console
$ grep -B2 -A5 "failed to deliver expiry notification" dist/server-aux-handlers-Dug4CJo6.js
});
})().catch((err) => {
log.error(`exec approvals: failed to deliver expiry notification for ${requestId}: ${String(err)}`);
});
}, expiresInMs);
timeoutId.unref?.();
```

### Gateway startup proof

The patched exec-approval forwarder loads successfully in the production gateway:

```console
$ timeout 8 node openclaw.mjs gateway
2026-05-21T11:10:25.297-07:00 [gateway] loading configuration...
2026-05-21T11:10:25.347-07:00 [gateway] starting...
2026-05-21T11:10:28.532-07:00 [gateway] agent model: openai/gpt-5.5 (thinking=medium, fast=off)
2026-05-21T11:10:28.533-07:00 [gateway] http server listening (8 plugins; 3.2s)
2026-05-21T11:10:29.017-07:00 [gateway] ready
2026-05-21T11:10:32.981-07:00 [shutdown] completed cleanly in 79ms
```

### Test suite (22 tests)

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/exec-approval-forwarder.test.ts
 ✓ |infra| exec-approval-forwarder.test.ts (22 tests) 35ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  22 passed (22)
```

### Key test: delete-before-delivery semantics

The test "deletes pending entry before starting expiry delivery" verifies that `pending.delete(requestId)` runs before `deliverToTargets`. During expiry delivery, it calls `handleResolved` on the same request; since the pending entry is already gone, no resolved notice is delivered (only 1 delivery call for the expiry notification):

```typescript
// During expiry delivery, handleResolved finds no pending entry
// because pending.delete ran before deliverToTargets
expect(deliver).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1); // only the expiry notification
```

Observed result after fix: The `.catch()` handler prevents unhandled rejections from the expiry delivery IIFE. The `pending.delete` stays before the await, keeping expiry finalization independent from channel delivery latency. All 22 tests pass (19 existing + 3 new).

What was not tested: Triggering an actual unexpected error that escapes `deliverToTargets`'s internal per-target catch in a live gateway session. The `.catch()` is a safety net for edge cases (like module initialization errors or future code changes that add throwing paths before `deliverToTargets`). The `deliverToTargets` function catches all per-target delivery errors internally, so under normal operation the outer `.catch()` does not fire.

## ClawSweeper P1 fix

**[P1] Finalize expiry before awaiting delivery** (exec-approval-forwarder.ts:577-582): Fixed. The previous version moved `pending.delete(requestId)` after `await deliverToTargets(...)`, keeping expired forwarder state alive during delivery. This version keeps the delete before the await (matching current main) and only adds `.catch()` to the fire-and-forget IIFE for unhandled rejection safety.

## Summary

target.title: fix(mattermost): forward mediaLocalRoots in handleAction for agent workspace file uploads
target.body:
## Summary

Mattermost's `handleAction` in `extensions/mattermost/src/channel.ts` did not destructure or forward the `mediaLocalRoots` parameter from its context to `sendMessageMattermost`. This caused agent workspace files (under `workspace-<agentId>`) to be silently rejected when sent via the message tool.

## Problem

When an agent uses the **message tool** (`action: "send"`) to send a file from its own workspace directory (e.g., `workspace-mattermost-mot/image.png`) to a Mattermost channel:

1. `executeSendAction` in `outbound-send-service.ts` correctly computes `mediaLocalRoots` (including the agent's workspace directory) and passes it to `dispatchChannelMessageAction`.
2. `dispatchChannelMessageAction` passes the full context (including `mediaLocalRoots`) to the Mattermost plugin's `handleAction`.
3. **Bug**: `handleAction` destructured only `{ action, params, cfg, accountId }` — ignoring `mediaLocalRoots`.
4. `sendMessageMattermost` was called without `mediaLocalRoots`, so `loadOutboundMediaFromUrl` fell back to `getDefaultMediaLocalRoots()`, which does **not** include `workspace-<agentId>` directories.
5. `assertLocalMediaAllowed` in `local-media-access.ts` explicitly rejects paths under `workspace-*` when using default roots.
6. The upload error was **silently caught** in `send.ts:428-436`, and the message was sent as plain text without the file attachment — no error was surfaced to the agent or user.

Other channel plugins (Discord, Telegram, Google Chat) already correctly destructure and forward `mediaLocalRoots` in their `handleAction` implementations.

## Fix

- Destructure `mediaLocalRoots` from the `handleAction` context parameter.
- Forward it to `sendMessageMattermost` in the send options.

## Verification

**Unit tests:**
- Added a new test case `"forwards mediaLocalRoots on send action with media"` that verifies `mediaLocalRoots` is passed through to `sendMessageMattermost` when `handleAction` processes a send with media.
- All 23 existing Mattermost channel tests continue to pass.

**Manual testing (dev gateway):**
- Configured a Mattermost bot with an agent bound to `workspace-mattermost-mot`.
- On `main` (without fix): agent sends message via message tool with `filePath` pointing to a file in the agent workspace → message arrives as **plain text only**, file attachment is silently dropped.
- On fix branch: same operation → message arrives with the **file attachment correctly uploaded and visible** in Mattermost.

## Files changed

- `extensions/mattermost/src/channel.ts` — add `mediaLocalRoots` to `handleAction` destructuring and `sendMessageMattermost` call
- `extensions/mattermost/src/channel.test.ts` — add test for `mediaLocalRoots` forwarding in send action

target.title: Fail closed sessions_send announce planning for zero-timeout sends
target.body:
## Summary

This PR finishes the fail-closed `sessions_send` announce migration on top of `v2026.3.28` and tightens the zero-timeout path.

It combines 3 commits:

- `46e57841d8` Fail closed sessions_send announce planning
- `4d28d078dc` Split sessions and subagents registry tests
- `64b6eb4db1` Fail closed zero-timeout sessions_send announce

## What changed

- migrated fail-closed announce-target planning into the current `sessions_send` flow
- preserved the stable branch's newer reply snapshot / stale-reply protections
- made `timeoutSeconds=0` strictly fail closed without blocking on `sessions.list`
- kept immediate external targets on the fast skip path
- restored real schema coverage for `sessions_send` / `sessions_spawn`
- added a minimal `createOpenClawTools()` registry smoke test without reintroducing the old hanging registry path

## Behavior

### `sessions_send`
- external channel-bound targets skip announce
- unknown targets fail closed
- zero-timeout sends do not wait on `sessions.list`, `agent.wait`, or `chat.history`
- zero-timeout sends only use immediately provable routing information
- metadata-backed routes that are only discoverable through lookup now fail closed at `timeoutSeconds=0`

### tests
- split registry-level `sessions` and `subagents` coverage
- added focused zero-timeout regressions
- restored real schema checks for `sessions_send.timeoutSeconds`
- restored real schema checks for `sessions_spawn.runTimeoutSeconds` and legacy `timeoutSeconds`

## Verification

```bash
pnpm test src/agents/tools/sessions-send-announce-guard.test.ts \
  src/agents/tools/sessions.test.ts \
  src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts \
  src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.test.ts \
  src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions-visibility.test.ts
```

Result:

- `Test Files 5 passed`
- `Tests 61 passed`

## Notes

- zero-timeout `sessions_send` now prefers truthful fail-closed delivery reporting over lookup-dependent announce behavior
- an independent review on the final diff found no remaining issues

- Possibly related: #55981

target.title: fix(codex): unsubscribe app-server thread after runs
target.body:
Summary
- release the main Codex app-server thread subscription after completed non-timeout turns
- release the subscription when `turn/start` fails after a thread has already been created
- add focused regression coverage for completed-turn and turn-start-failure cleanup

Verification
- `pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt-thread-cleanup.test.ts`
- `pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts -- -t "restarts the app-server once|tolerates a second app-server close" --reporter=verbose`
- `git diff --check -- extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt-thread-cleanup.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Repeated Gateway-owned Codex app-server turns with Chrome DevTools MCP should release the main thread subscription after non-timeout completion/failure and should not accumulate `chrome-devtools-mcp` / `npx` sidecars in the short repeated-turn window.
- Real environment tested: Local Linux OpenClaw checkout, Node 22.22.2, isolated Gateway on `127.0.0.1:19743`, isolated state/config under `/tmp/openclaw-84413-repro`, headless Chrome on `127.0.0.1:19744`, Chrome DevTools MCP configured, `codex/gpt-5.5`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Started isolated headless Chrome, started isolated `pnpm openclaw gateway run --allow-unconfigured --cli-backend-logs --port 19743`, ran two `pnpm openclaw agent --agent dev --session-id issue-84413-live-proof ... --json --timeout 300` turns, captured process snapshots before/after.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal transcript:
  ```text
  $ curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:19743/readyz
  {"ready":true,"failing":[],"eventLoop":{"degraded":false}}

  First completed turn:
  runId: a56c9a0f-e22e-4201-af6b-007eff90b249
  status: ok
  toolSummary.calls: 4
  toolSummary.tools: codex.list_mcp_resources, codex.list_mcp_resource_templates, bash
  finalAssistantVisibleText: about:blank

  Second completed turn:
  runId: 08e8d325-12b7-4f90-9a03-c3be01ba6f14
  status: ok
  toolSummary.calls: 1
  toolSummary.tools: bash
  finalAssistantVisibleText: about:blank

  Post-second-turn process snapshot:
  one Gateway-owned Codex app-server wrapper process
  one Gateway-owned Codex native app-server child process
  no chrome-devtools-mcp process
  no npx -y chrome-devtools process

  Cleanup verification after stopping isolated Gateway and Chrome:
  no 19743 gateway process
  no 19744 Chrome fixture process
  no repo-local Codex app-server proof process
  ```
- Observed result after fix: Two repeated real Gateway turns completed successfully in the isolated setup. The short-window process snapshot did not accumulate `chrome-devtools-mcp` / `npx` sidecars, and all proof processes were gone after cleanup.
- What was not tested: The 30+ minute upstream Codex idle unload/reap delay was not waited out; this patch releases the thread subscription but does not force-kill the app-server immediately.

Fixes #84413

target.body:
## Summary
- surface generic channel runtime warnings for disconnected, stale, stuck, and restart-pending states
- add inbound delivery telemetry for received, dispatch started/completed, session turn created, and processed events
- show delivery counters, transport liveness, multi-gateway listener warnings, and no-recent-agent-activity warnings in status output
- normalize provider allowlist catalog-loading flags accepted from plugin auth prompts

Fixes #49577

## Tests
- `corepack pnpm test -- src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/dispatch.test.ts extensions/diagnostics-prometheus/src/service.test.ts extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.test.ts src/infra/channels-status-issues.test.ts src/commands/status-all/diagnosis.test.ts src/commands/status-all/report-lines.test.ts src/logging/diagnostic-stability.test.ts test/scripts/parallels-smoke-model.test.ts src/plugins/provider-validation.test.ts src/commands/channels.surfaces-signal-runtime-errors-channels-status-output.test.ts` - passed 8 Vitest shards in 29.58s
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts test/scripts/parallels-smoke-model.test.ts src/plugins/provider-validation.ts extensions/diagnostics-prometheus/src/service.ts extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.ts CHANGELOG.md`
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: connected gateway inbound delivery now records payload-safe delivery diagnostics and exposes them through the `diagnostics.stability` Gateway RPC used by status diagnostics.
- Real environment tested: local macOS 26.4.1 arm64 checkout, isolated `pr72724` OpenClaw profile, local loopback gateway on `ws://127.0.0.1:19724`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - Started a real local gateway from the PR branch: `corepack pnpm openclaw --profile pr72724 gateway run --port 19724 --bind loopback --allow-unconfigured --auth token --token <redacted>`
  - Approved the isolated profile's local CLI device for the requested operator write scope.
  - Sent a real WebChat inbound delivery through the gateway: `corepack pnpm openclaw --profile pr72724 gateway call chat.send --url ws://127.0.0.1:19724 --token <redacted> --json --timeout 15000 --params '{"sessionKey":"main","message":"Reply exactly OK.","idempotencyKey":"pr72724-proof-4","timeoutMs":1000}'`
  - Queried live stability diagnostics: `corepack pnpm openclaw --profile pr72724 gateway stability --url ws://127.0.0.1:19724 --token <redacted> --json`

- Evidence after fix: redacted terminal excerpts from the connected local gateway run:

```json
{
  "runId": "pr72724-proof-4",
  "status": "started"
}
```

```json

  "type": "message.received",
  "channel": "webchat",

```

```

- Observed result after fix: a connected gateway/channel inbound path accepted `chat.send`, returned a started run id, and the live `diagnostics.stability` RPC reported the new payload-free `message.received` and dispatch telemetry events from the inbound dispatch boundary.
- What was not tested: external Discord/Signal/Telegram live transport delivery; this proof uses the connected built-in WebChat channel path on a local gateway.

target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Workspace `.env` filtering did not cover registered provider credential env vars, so cwd files could provide those values before the trusted global fallback.
- Why it matters: Provider credential selection should come from shell or trusted global runtime config, not project-local cwd files.
- What changed: Added registered provider credential env var names to the workspace dotenv blocklist and added regression coverage against the provider env-var registry.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): Trusted global `.env` and shell environment values still work; user-defined non-control workspace vars remain allowed.

AI-assisted: yes.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- N/A
- [x] This PR addresses a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Registered provider credential env vars from cwd `.env` are ignored, and trusted global `.env` values are retained.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout using temp cwd and state-dir fixtures, installed as a local node package. Also used openclaw@2026.5.12 as the public version with the issue to be fixed.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/dotenv.test.ts` and from the regular home and `evil/` subdirectory run:
```

'
```
- What was not tested: A complete local installation and execution of OpenClaw, instead the code changed was tested with a simulated attacker controlled .env file in a subfolder, "evil".
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Real proof by reproducing the security issue on ubuntu latest with `openclaw@2026.5.12` per researcher findings and proof-of-concept. Behavior before patch with `openclaw@2026.5.12`:
```
